channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump administration vows crackdown on Chinese firms 'exploiting' U.S. AI models
item.description: The Trump administration is vowing to crack down on foreign tech companies' exploitation of U.S. artificial intelligence models, singling out China at a time that country is narrowing the gap with the U.S. in the AI race.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration is vowing to crack down on foreign tech companies' exploitation of U.S. artificial intelligence models, singling out China at a time that country is narrowing the gap with the U.S. in the AI race.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: EU approves a $106 billion loan package to help Ukraine after Hungary lifts its veto
item.description: The European Union on Thursday approved a $106-billion loan package to help Ukraine meet its economic and military needs for two years, ending months of political deadlock.
item.content:encoded:
The European Union on Thursday approved a $106-billion loan package to help Ukraine meet its economic and military needs for two years, ending months of political deadlock.

(Image credit: Petros Karadjias)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: French police probe suspected weather device tampering after odd Polymarket bet
item.description: The incident is the latest eyebrow-raising bet on Polymarket, as allegations of rigging and manipulation continue to haunt the popular prediction market site.
item.content:encoded:
The incident is the latest eyebrow-raising bet on Polymarket, as allegations of rigging and manipulation continue to haunt the popular prediction market site.

(Image credit: Riccardo Milani)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Airlines in Europe slash thousands of flights as Iran war cuts jet fuel supplies
item.description: The soaring cost of jet fuel is forcing European airlines to cancel tens of thousands of flights, while energy authorities warned of a possible jet fuel shortage if supplies aren't replenished soon.
item.content:encoded:
The soaring cost of jet fuel is forcing European airlines to cancel tens of thousands of flights, while energy authorities warned of a possible jet fuel shortage if supplies aren't replenished soon.

(Image credit: Isabelle Souriment + Hans Lucas)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: Tensions rise in two ceasefires in the Middle East. And, the Navy secretary ousted
item.description: Tensions are rising in the Middle East as shaky ceasefire agreements between the U.S., Israel and Iran, and Lebanon and Israel, are tested. And, the Secretary of the Navy is out of the role.
item.content:encoded:
Tensions are rising in the Middle East as shaky ceasefire agreements between the U.S., Israel and Iran, and Lebanon and Israel, are tested. And, the Secretary of the Navy is out of the role.

(Image credit: Andrew Cabellero-Reynolds)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Trump announces a 3-week extension of the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
item.description: President Trump said Thursday that Israel and Lebanon have agreed to extend their ceasefire for three weeks after the two sides met at the White House for a high-level negotiation.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump said Thursday that Israel and Lebanon have agreed to extend their ceasefire for three weeks after the two sides met at the White House for a high-level negotiation.

(Image credit: Mohammed Zaatari)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Peace talks between U.S. and Iran at a standstill as Trump extends ceasefire
item.description: President Trump has extended the ceasefire, but Iran says it's not enough if the naval blockade is still in place.
item.content:encoded: President Trump has extended the ceasefire, but Iran says it's not enough if the naval blockade is still in place.
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: How TikTok is driving American expats to Southeast Asia
item.description: Americans who moved to Vietnam and Thailand say their lives are now lower-stress and lower-cost. But glamorous videos on TikTok don't tell the whole story.
item.content:encoded:
Americans who moved to Vietnam and Thailand say their lives are now lower-stress and lower-cost. But glamorous videos on TikTok don't tell the whole story.

(Image credit: Linh Pham)
item.dc:creator: Windsor Johnston

item.title: World Press Photo announces Photo of the Year 2026
item.description: The executive director of World Press Photo said the image shows the inconsolable grief of children losing their father in a place built for justice. It is a stark and necessary record of family separation following the U.S. reform policies.
item.content:encoded:
The executive director of World Press Photo said the image shows the inconsolable grief of children losing their father in a place built for justice. It is a stark and necessary record of family separation following the U.S. reform policies.

(Image credit: Carol Guzy)
item.dc:creator: Ava Pukatch

item.title: Candidates vying for U.N. secretary-general role face questions
item.description: Who will replace U.N. Secretary-General Antonio Guterres when he steps down this year? Four candidates field questions from member states this week at U.N. headquarters.
item.content:encoded: Who will replace U.N. Secretary-General Antonio Guterres when he steps down this year? Four candidates field questions from member states this week at U.N. headquarters.
item.dc:creator: Michele Kelemen
